description: Example vulnerability database demonstrating custom patterns for Soroban contracts

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Authentication for Admin Functions
vulnerabilities.description: Administrative functions that modify contract state without authentication checks can be exploited by attackers to gain unauthorized control
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Always verify caller authentication in admin functions using require_auth() or require_auth_for_args() before modifying storage

vulnerabilities.name: Potential Reentrancy in External Call
vulnerabilities.description: External contract calls before state updates can lead to reentrancy attacks where the called contract calls back into the original contract
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Update storage state before making external contract calls. Follow the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern

vulnerabilities.name: Potential Integer Overflow in Balance Update
vulnerabilities.description: Arithmetic operations on token balances without overflow checking can lead to wrapping behavior and unexpected results
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use checked arithmetic (checked_add, checked_sub) and handle None cases explicitly. Consider whether saturating arithmetic is appropriate for your use case
